allow: Bash(python3 -c "import os; print\('CPU cores \(logical\):', os.cpu_count\(\)\)" 2>/dev/null; nproc; lscpu | grep -E "^CPU\\\(s\\\)|Thread|Core|Model name")

Bash(python3 -c "
lines = open\('siam_seg_anchor.py'\).readlines\(\)
out = []
for i, line in enumerate\(lines\):
    n = i + 1  # 1-indexed line number
    if 595 <= n <= 599:
        # Replace old header with new one
        if n == 595:
            out.append\('# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\\n'\)
            out.append\('# ORIGINAL main\(\) – commented out.\\n'\)
            out.append\('# To revert: uncomment this block and comment out the new main\(\) below.\\n'\)
            out.append\('# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\\n'\)
        # skip original header lines 596-599
        continue
    elif 601 <= n <= 1052:
        # Comment out the old main body
        # Also change _main_original back to main so uncommenting just works
        text = line.replace\('def _main_original\(\):', 'def main\(\):'\)
        if text.strip\(\):
            out.append\('# ' + text\)
        else:
            out.append\('#\\n'\)
    else:
        out.append\(line\)
open\('siam_seg_anchor.py', 'w'\).writelines\(out\)
print\('Done – lines 601-1052 commented out'\)
")
Bash(python3 -c "import py_compile; py_compile.compile\('siam_seg_anchor.py', doraise=True\); print\('Syntax OK'\)")

Bash(python3 -c "
import ast
source = open\('siam_seg_anchor.py'\).read\(\)
tree = ast.parse\(source\)

# Count functions and check for main
funcs = [n.name for n in ast.walk\(tree\) if isinstance\(n, \(ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef\)\)]
classes = [n.name for n in ast.walk\(tree\) if isinstance\(n, ast.ClassDef\)]
print\(f'Syntax OK'\)
print\(f'Functions: {len\(funcs\)}'\)
print\(f'Classes: {classes}'\)
print\(f'Has main\(\): {\\"main\\" in funcs}'\)

# Count lines
lines = source.count\('\\n'\) + 1
print\(f'Total lines: {lines}'\)
")

Bash(python3 -c "
import cv2
cap = cv2.VideoCapture\('/tmp/track_test_output.mp4'\)
for f in [0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 250, 299]:
    cap.set\(cv2.CAP_PROP_POS_FRAMES, f\)
    ok, frame = cap.read\(\)
    if ok:
        cv2.imwrite\(f'/tmp/track_f{f:03d}.jpg', frame\)
cap.release\(\)
print\('Saved sample frames'\)
" 2>&1)
